defs: A young pig, or a litter of pigs.

records.ety: [OE. fer, feor, AS. feor; akin to OS. fer, D. ver, OHG. ferro, adv., G. fern, a., Icel. fjarri, Dan. fjirn, Sw. fjerran, adv., Goth. faīrra, adv., Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/ beyond, Skr. paras, adv., far, and prob. to L. per through, and E. prefix for-, as in forgive, and also to fare. Cf. Farther, Farthest.]

defs: Distant in any direction; not near; remote; mutually separated by a wide space or extent.
quotes: They said, . . . We be come from a far country.
The nations far and near contend in choice.

defs: Remote from purpose; contrary to design or wishes; as, far be it from me to justify cruelty.
Remote in affection or obedience; at a distance, morally or spiritually; t enmity with; alienated.
quotes: They that are far from thee ahsll perish.

defs: Widely different in nature or quality; opposite in character.
quotes: He was far from ill looking, though he thought himself still farther.

defs: The more distant of two; as, the far side (called also off side) of a horse, that is, the right side, or the one opposite to the rider when he mounts.
notes: ☞ The distinction between the adjectival and adverbial use of far is sometimes not easily discriminated.

defs: To a great extent or distance of space; widely; as, we are separated far from each other.
To a great distance in time from any point; remotely; as, he pushed his researches far into antiquity.
In great part; as, the day is far spent.
In a great proportion; by many degrees; very much; deeply; greatly.
quotes: Who can find a virtuous woman ? for her price is far above rubies.
notes: ☞ Far often occurs in self-explaining compounds, such as far-extended, far-reaching, far-spread.
